page: OVHcloud c2-30 is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel architecture based on the Haswell microarchitecture, operating at a base frequency of 3.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, meaning hyperthreading is disabled with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.75 GB of RAM per core, and includes 200 GB of local SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core, reach average levels. Memory bandwidth reads are average at 57.3 GB/sec uncached, though uncached memory write bandwidth is weaker at 23.36 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks show mixed results, with weak single-threaded gzip compression but average multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

Given the dedicated core allocation and average multi-threaded performance, the c2-30 is suited for parallelizable workloads that do not rely heavily on single-thread speed. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, and database operations, as evidenced by average Passmark database and Redis SET performance. It can also handle light, small-scale LLM inference tasks. However, it is less suitable for single-threaded applications or heavy cryptographic processing due to architectural limitations.
description: OVHcloud c2-30 is a compute-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 8 dedicated physical Intel cores running at 3.0 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. Lacking hyperthreading, the instance provides exactly 8 vCPUs alongside 30.0 GB of RAM and 200 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark results indicate weak single-core performance, but average multi-core capabilities, making it a viable option for parallelized tasks. Memory read bandwidth is average at 57.3 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is weaker at 23.36 GB/sec. This hardware profile is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, and database operations, though it is constrained in single-threaded and cryptographic tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud c2-30 is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated Intel cores, 30 GB of RAM, and 200 GB of local SSD storage, delivering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute instance with local SSD storage, optimized for parallel workloads and multi-threaded processing.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical Intel cores with no hyperthreading at 3.0 GHz
30.0 GB of system memory and 200 GB of local SSD storage
Average multi-core performance suitable for parallel processing
KVM-based virtualization with dedicated CPU allocation
Best suited for static web serving, databases, and multi-threaded compilation
